title: Colo: A Gorilla's Groundbreaking Birth
story: A tiny gorilla named Colo made history in 1956. Born at the Columbus Zoo in Ohio on December 22, she was the first gorilla ever bred in captivity. Her story inspired better conservation efforts for gorillas in the wild too.

title: Icy Blast Freezes America
story: Brrr! A fierce cold wave swept across America in 1989, turning December 22 into a frosty wonderland. Temperatures plummeted to bone-chilling lows, with Tulsa shivering at -6°F and Pittsburgh at -12°F. Denver dropped to a frosty -18°F, while Kansas City hit a frigid -23°F. The coldest spots? title: Beethoven's Musical Marathon
story: Imagine a concert hall buzzing with excitement. On December 22, 1808, the audience in Vienna, Austria, was about to witness something extraordinary. title: Meat Tax Sparks Dutch Revolt
story: Sizzling sausages and juicy steaks caused quite a stir in 1731! On December 22, Dutch citizens took to the streets in protest. Why? A new tax on meat had people fired up.
